# "Semi-Infinite Solid — Interactive Demo" {.unnumbered}

A semi-infinite solid ($x \ge 0$) starts at uniform temperature $T_i$.
At $t = 0$, something happens at the surface — the three cases differ only in *what* that something is.
Each plot shows temperature profiles at five time snapshots.

| Case | Surface BC | Key parameter |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| I    | $T(0,t) = T_s$ | prescribed temperature |
| II   | $-k\,\partial T/\partial x\big|_0 = q_0''$ | prescribed heat flux |
| III  | $-k\,\partial T/\partial x\big|_0 = h(T_\infty - T(0))$ | convection |

---

## Interactive demo {#sec-demo}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Special functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function erf(x) {
  if (x < 0) return -erf(-x);
  const t = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.3275911 * x);
  const p = t * (0.254829592 + t * (-0.284496736 + t * (1.421413741
          + t * (-1.453152027 + t * 1.061405429))));
  return 1.0 - p * Math.exp(-x * x);
}

function erfc(x) { return 1 - erf(x); }

// erfcx(u) = exp(u²)·erfc(u) — bounded for all u, needed for Case III
function erfcx(u) {
  if (u < 0) return 2 * Math.exp(u * u) - erfcx(-u);
  if (u < 5) return Math.exp(u * u) * erfc(u);
  const t = 1 / (u * u);
  return (1 / Math.sqrt(Math.PI)) / u
       * (1 - t*0.5 + t*t*0.75 - t*t*t*1.875 + t*t*t*t*6.5625);
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  // ── Inputs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const T_i_in  = Inputs.range([-20,  20], {value: -5,  step: 1,    label: "Tᵢ (°C)"});
  const alp_in  = Inputs.range([-7.5,-5.7],{value:-6.3, step: 0.05, label: "log₁₀(α)"});
  const k_in    = Inputs.range([0.1,  5.0],{value: 1.0, step: 0.1,  label: "k (W/m·K)"});
  const Ts_in   = Inputs.range([-30,  40], {value: 15,  step: 1,    label: html`<em>T</em><sub>s</sub> (°C)`});
  const q0_in   = Inputs.range([0,  1000], {value:300,  step: 10,   label: html`<em>q</em><sub>0</sub>″ (W/m²)`});
  const h_in    = Inputs.range([1,   100], {value: 25,  step: 1,    label: html`<em>h</em> (W/m²·K)`});
  const Tinf_in = Inputs.range([-30,  40], {value: 20,  step: 1,    label: "T∞ (°C)"});

  // ── Plot placeholder divs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const p1 = document.createElement("div");
  const p2 = document.createElement("div");
  const p3 = document.createElement("div");

  // ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const times_s     = [7, 30, 90, 180, 365].map(d => d * 86400);
  const time_labels = ["7 days", "30 days", "90 days", "180 days", "1 year"];
  const time_colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"];

  // ── Render ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  function render() {
    const T_i  = T_i_in.value,  alp  = Math.pow(10, alp_in.value);
    const kk   = k_in.value,    T_s  = Ts_in.value;
    const q0   = q0_in.value,   hh   = h_in.value,  Tinf = Tinf_in.value;

    const x_max = Math.min(30, Math.max(2, 4 * Math.sqrt(alp * 365 * 86400)));
    const xs = Array.from({length: 300}, (_, i) => i / 299 * x_max);

    function rows(fn) {
      return times_s.flatMap((t_s, j) =>
        xs.map(x => ({ x, T: fn(x, t_s), time: time_labels[j] }))
      );
    }

    const rows1 = rows((x, t) => {
      const eta = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alp * t));
      return T_s + (T_i - T_s) * erf(eta);
    });

    const rows2 = rows((x, t) => {
      const eta = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alp * t));
      return T_i
        + (2 * q0 * Math.sqrt(alp * t / Math.PI) / kk) * Math.exp(-eta * eta)
        - (q0 * x / kk) * erfc(eta);
    });

    const rows3 = rows((x, t) => {
      const eta  = x / (2 * Math.sqrt(alp * t));
      const beta = hh * Math.sqrt(alp * t) / kk;
      return T_i + (Tinf - T_i)
        * (erfc(eta) - Math.exp(-eta * eta) * erfcx(eta + beta));
    });

    function plot(data) {
      return Plot.plot({
        width: 315, height: 300,
        marginLeft: 44, marginBottom: 40, marginTop: 10,
        style: { fontFamily: "Georgia, serif", fontSize: "11px" },
        x: { label: "x (m) →", domain: [0, x_max], axis: "bottom" },
        y: { label: "↑ T (°C)", axis: "left" },
        color: { domain: time_labels, range: time_colors, legend: false },
        marks: [
          Plot.gridX({stroke: "#e0e0e0"}),
          Plot.gridY({stroke: "#e0e0e0"}),
          Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#aaa", strokeDasharray: "4,3", strokeWidth: 1}),
          Plot.line(data, {x: "x", y: "T", stroke: "time", strokeWidth: 2}),
        ]
      });
    }

    p1.replaceChildren(plot(rows1));
    p2.replaceChildren(plot(rows2));
    p3.replaceChildren(plot(rows3));
  }

  for (const inp of [T_i_in, alp_in, k_in, Ts_in, q0_in, h_in, Tinf_in])
    inp.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();

  // ── Shared legend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const legendEl = Plot.plot({
    width: 969, height: 28,
    marginTop: 0, marginBottom: 0, marginLeft: 0, marginRight: 0,
    color: { domain: ["7 days","30 days","90 days","180 days","1 year"],
             range:  ["#1f77b4","#ff7f0e","#2ca02c","#d62728","#9467bd"],
             legend: true },
    marks: []
  });

  // ── Layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  return html`<div style="font-family:Georgia,serif">

    <div style="margin-bottom:10px">
      <div style="font-size:0.8em; font-weight:bold; color:#666;
                  text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.05em; margin-bottom:5px">
        Shared parameters
      </div>
      <div style="display:flex; gap:24px; flex-wrap:wrap">
        ${T_i_in} ${alp_in} ${k_in}
      </div>
    </div>

    ${legendEl}

    <div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,315px);
                gap:12px; margin-top:8px">

      <div>
        <div style="font-weight:bold; font-size:0.75em; margin-bottom:4px">
          Case I — prescribed <em>T</em><sub>s</sub>
        </div>
        ${Ts_in}
        ${p1}
      </div>

      <div>
        <div style="font-weight:bold; font-size:0.75em; margin-bottom:4px">
          Case II — prescribed <em>q</em><sub>0</sub>″
        </div>
        ${q0_in}
        ${p2}
      </div>

      <div>
        <div style="font-weight:bold; font-size:0.75em; margin-bottom:4px">
          Case III — convection <em>h</em>
        </div>
        ${h_in} ${Tinf_in}
        ${p3}
      </div>

    </div>
  </div>`;
}
```

---

## What to look for {#sec-observations}

**Case I — error function profile, surface pinned at Tₛ.**
The surface is always exactly $T_s$ and far away the temperature is still $T_i$.

**Case II — surface temperature grows without bound.**
There is no target surface temperature; instead energy arrives at a constant rate $q_0''$.
The surface temperature grows $\sim\!\sqrt{t}$, which is physically unrealistic at long times. Notice that deeper in the solid the profiles look qualitatively similar to Case I.

**Case III — convection slows the approach.**
The surface doesn't immediately reach $T_\infty$... it approaches it gradually.
A small $h$ means the convective resistance dominates: little heat is transferred and the solid barely warms.
A large $h$ drives Case III toward Case I — the surface is heated so efficiently that it behaves like a prescribed temperature.

---

## Worked example — Case I thaw depth {#sec-worked}

Frozen soil ($\alpha = 5 \times 10^{-7}\ \text{m}^2/\text{s}$, $T_i = -5\,^\circ\text{C}$) has its surface suddenly raised to $T_s = 15\,^\circ\text{C}$.
How deep does the 0°C thaw front reach after 90 days?

We need to determine the location where $T = 0\,^\circ\text{C}$$, so we solve for $\eta$ such that $\text{erf}(\eta) = T-T_s/(T_i - T_s)=0.75$. Can ask [Wolfram Alpha](https://www.wolframalpha.com/input?i=solve+erf%28x%29%3D0.75), and will determine $\eta=0.813$. Then the thaw depth is

$$
x = 2\sqrt{\alpha t}\cdot\eta = 2\sqrt{(5\times10^{-7}\ \text{m}^2/\text{s})\,(90\ \text{days}\times 86{,}400\ \text{s/day})}\times 0.8134 \approx 3.21\ \text{m}.
$$

Verify this against the "90 days" curve in Case I above (with $T_s = 15\,^\circ\text{C}$, $T_i = -5\,^\circ\text{C}$): the curve should cross the dashed $T=0$ line at about $x = 3.2\ \text{m}$.
